peer_exemplars.reasoning: High openness and very high extraversion, combined with low agreeableness and strong anti-domination values, drive support for transparency as a check on opaque power, but moderate trust in institutions tempers it to a clear but not maximal stance.

Her extreme libertarian anti-censorship orientation (98.59) and high anti-domination moral foundation (96.97) drive strong support for making platform algorithms transparent, but moderate trust in science (57.81) tempers the score slightly.

High trust in institutions and science, combined with very low agreeableness and a strong anti-egalitarian streak, leads to moderate support for transparency as a check on corporate power, but skepticism of state overreach tempers enthusiasm.

Low trust in government oversight combined with strong anti-censorship civil liberties orientation leads to mild opposition, seeing mandated transparency as a step toward authoritarian control rather than user empowerment.
High conscientiousness and openness support transparency, but low trust in institutions and a libertarian-authoritarian tilt toward state control skepticism moderate enthusiasm.

High openness and libertarian anti-censorship orientation drive support for transparency, but low agreeableness and high trust in institutions temper enthusiasm for mandated disclosure.
